# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment that mounts host directories for our logging agent. The pod needs access to /var/log and /var/lib/docker to collect logs from all containers. This is for our centralized logging system that runs as a DaemonSet.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1beta2 # Specify the API version you are using (apps v1beta in this case) 
kind: Deployment   
metadata:  
 name: log-agent      
specs:    
 replicas : 3            
 selector:     
  matchLabels:         
   app : myApp            # The label that select the pod(i.e., which Pod to direct traffic) - Here it's 'myapp'.    This is necessary as Deployment manages a ReplicaSet, and you need labels in order for Kubernetes to know where your replicas are
 template:      
  metadata :   # Specify the pod details (metadata of one replica/pod instance being created)    		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields        		       
  labels:          # Label for the pod as it relates to Pod (i.e., identifying which group of replicas has that specific object)      		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
   app : myApp     # Here it's 'myapp'.  This is used for identifying the pod as a part of this set (Deployment)		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
   version : v1     # Specify your label value, e.g., myapp_V2 (the pod's name and the tag)		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
   tier : logagent  # Here it is 'logAgent'. (The specific logging agent for this pod instance)		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
   name: myPod       # Specify the Pod's label value, e., "myapp_pod1" (the pod instance identifier)		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
   annotations:        # Additional information to provide for debugging, logging or monitoring – not required by K8S itself		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
     logagent: "docker" # You may provide key value pairs in the annotation as per your requirement for logging agent configuration – not required by K8S itself		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
   ports:                # Define the port(s) to expose from your pod as a set of named Ports, ie., you'd specify an exposed container service on which traffic will be redirected – not required by K8S itself		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
   - name: http           # Specify the port's label value, e., "myapp_http" (the service instance identifier)		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
     containerPort: 80   # Define the port number to expose from your pod as a specific service on which traffic will be redirected		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
     protocol: TCP       # Specify the port's label value, e., "myapp_http" (the service instance identifier)		# More info about each field can be found below    https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/api-version/core/v3#metaforaofields
